questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f$ be a continuous and differentiable function in $\left(x_1, x_2\right)$ and the following conditions hold for it.
$$
\left\{\begin{array}{l}
f(x) f^{\prime}(x) \geq x \sqrt{1-(f(x))^4} \\
\lim _{x \rightarrow x_1^{+}}(f(x))^2=1 \\
\lim _{x \rightarrow x_2^{-}}(f(x))^2=\frac{1}{2}
\end{array}\right.
$$

If the minimum value of $\left(x_1^2-x_2^2\right)$ is equal to $\frac{\pi}{k}$ where $k \in N$, then find the value of $k$.
[Note: $f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{d f(x)}{d x}$ ]
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \int_{x_1}^{x_2} \frac{f(x) f^{\prime}(x)}{\sqrt{1-(f(x))^4}} d x \geq \int_{x_1}^{x_2} x d x \\
& \left.\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1} f^2(x)\right]_{x_1}^{x_2} \geq \frac{1}{2}\left(x_2^2-x_1^2\right) \\
& \sin ^{-1} f^2\left(x_2^{-}\right)-\sin ^{-1} f^2\left(x_1^{+}\right) \geq\left(x_2^2-x_1^2\right) \\
& \frac{\pi}{6}-\frac{\pi}{2} \geq\left(x_2^2-x_1^2\right) \\
& \frac{\pi}{3} \leq\left(x_1^2-x_2^2\right) \\
& \therefore \quad x_1^2-x_2^2 \geq \frac{\pi}{3} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence minimum value of $x_1^2-x_2^2$ is $\frac{\pi}{3} \Rightarrow k=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=6-3^{(\sqrt{3} \sin x-\cos x)}$ and $g(x)=\operatorname{sgn}\left(x^2+2 p x+4 p+f(x)\right)$; if $g(x)$ is continuous $\forall x \in R$, then find the sum of all possible integral values of $p$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x) \geq-3 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+2 p x+4 p+f(x) \geq x^2+2 p x+4 p-3>0 \forall x \in R \\ & \Rightarrow p^2-4 p+3 < 0 \Rightarrow 1 \leq p \leq 3\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let a function $f: R \rightarrow R$ be defined as $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}\sin x, & a < x \leq b \\ |x-c|-d, & x \leq a \text { or } x>b\end{array}\right.$ If $f(x)$ is derivable $\forall x \in R$, find the minimum value of $|[a+b+c+d]|$. [Note: ['] denotes the greatest integer function.]
questions.solution:
$$
f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}
\sin x, & a < x \leq b \\
c-x-d, & x \in(-\infty, a] \\
x-c-d, & x \in(b, \infty)
\end{array}\right.
$$
[Note: $C$ must lies between $a$ and $b$.]
Now,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left.\begin{array}{l}
f^{\prime}\left(a^{+}\right)=\cos a \\
f^{\prime}\left(a^{-}\right)=-1
\end{array}\right] \quad \therefore \cos a=-1 \Rightarrow a=(2 n+1) \pi, n \in I \\
& \left.\begin{array}{l}
f^{\prime}\left(b^{-}\right)=\cos b \\
f^{\prime}\left(b^{+}\right)=1
\end{array}\right] \quad \therefore \cos b=1 \Rightarrow b=2 m \pi, m \in I \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\therefore & \sin a=0=\sin b \\
\therefore & f(a)=0=f(b)
\end{array}
$$
and
$$
\text { Coordinate } \Rightarrow f\left(a^{+}\right)=f\left(a^{-}\right) \Rightarrow 0=c-a-d
$$
$$
\Rightarrow f\left(b^{+}\right)=f\left(b^{-}\right) \Rightarrow b-c-d=0 \Rightarrow b=c+d
$$
$$
\therefore|[a+b+c+d]|=|[a+2 b]|=[(2 n+1) \pi+4 m \pi]=|[(2 n+1+4 m) \pi]|_{\text {min. }}=4
$$

Which occur at $n=-1$ and $m=0$ since $a < b$ (note this point).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=\{x+\sin x\}+[x-\sin x]+[x]$ where $[y]$ and $\{y\}$ denote greatest integer function and fractional part function of $y$ respectively, then find the number of points of discontinuity in $[0, \pi]$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
f(x) & =x+\sin x-[x+\sin x]+[x-\sin x]+[x] \\
x+\sin x & =0,1,2,3 \Rightarrow x=0, \alpha_1, \alpha_2, \alpha_3 \\
x-\sin x & =0,1,2,3 \Rightarrow x=0, \beta_1, \beta_2, \beta_3
\end{aligned}
$$
$f$ is continuous at $x=0$, but discontinuous at $\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \alpha_3, \beta_1, \beta_2, \beta_3, 1,2,3$
$\therefore$ Number of points of discontinuity $=9$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow R$ be a function defined by $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}x^3+2 x^2+x+c, & \text { if } x \leq b \\ e^x, & \text { if } x>b\end{array}\right.$, where $b$ and $c$ are integers. If $f(x)$ is differentiable $\forall x \in R$, then find the value of $(b+c)$.
questions.solution:
$$
b=0 \text { and } c=1
$$

Note that both $x^3+2 x^2+x+c$ and $e^x$ are differentiable in their domain.
So make the function differentiable at $x=b$.
Since $f$ is differentiable at $x=b$.
$$
\begin{gathered}
\text { L.H.D. }=\text { R.H.D. }(\text { at } x=b) \\
\qquad 3 b^2+4 b+1=e^b
\end{gathered}
$$
$b$ is an integer, so LHS of the equation will always be an integer. However RHS will be an integer only if $b=0$.
If $b=0$, LHS $=$ RHS, so $b=0$ is the solution to this equation.
Since $f$ is differentiable at $x=b$, it is implied that it is also continuous at $x=b$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
\lim _{x \rightarrow b^{-}} f(x) & =f(b)=\lim _{x \rightarrow b^{+}} f(x) \\
b^3+2 b^2+b+c & =e^b
\end{aligned}
$$
when $b=0$, we get $c=e^0=1$
so the answer is $0+1=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Let $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{\tan ^3 x+\tan ^2 x-a \tan x-a}{e^{(\operatorname{ian} x-2)}-1}, & x \in(0, \pi / 2)-x_0 \\ b, & x \equiv x_0\end{array}\right.$. If $f$ is continuous in $(0, \pi / 2)$ then find the value of $\left[a+b+x_0\right]$. [Note: $[y]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $y$.]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
The number of points of discontinuity of $f(x)$ in $[0,2]$ where
$f(x)= \begin{cases}{[\cos \pi x],} & x \leq 1 \\ |2 x-3|[x-2], & x\gt1\end{cases}$, ([.] denotes the greatest integer function), are __________ .
questions.solution: The function is discontinuous at $\mathrm{x}=0, \frac{1}{2}$ and 2 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{cc}x^3 & \text { if } x \lt 1 \\ a x^2+b x+c & \text { if } x \geq 1\end{array}\right.$. If $f^{\prime \prime}(1)$ exist then find the value of $a^3+b^3+c^3$.
questions.solution:
For continuity at $x=1$ we leave $f\left(1^{-}\right)=1$ and $f\left(1^{+}\right)=a+b+c$
$\therefore a+b+c=1...(1)$
$f^{\prime}(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}
3 x^2 & \text { if } x \lt 1 \\
2 a x+b & \text { if } x \geq 1
\end{array}\right.$
for continuity of $\mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})$ at $\mathrm{x}=1$
$f^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=3 ; f^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right)=2 a+b$
hence $2 a+b=3....(2)$
$\begin{aligned}
& f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\left[\begin{array}{cc}
6 x & \text { if } x \lt 1 \\
2 a & \text { if } x \geq 1
\end{array}\right. \\
& f^{\prime \prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=6 ; f^{\prime \prime}\left(1^{+}\right)=2 a \\
& \therefore 2 a=6 \\
& \Rightarrow a=3
\end{aligned}$
from (2), b = - 3
from (2), c = 1
Hence $a=3, b=-3 ; c=1$
$\therefore \sum \mathrm{a}^3=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{lr}e^x\left(\frac{e^{n x}-1}{e^x-1}+x^3\right), & x \neq 0 \\ k, & x=0\end{array}\right.$
(where $n \in N$ ) be a differentiable function and if $f^{\prime \prime \prime}(0)=1302$, then find the value of $(k+n)$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & k=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{e^x\left(e^{n x}-1\right)}{e^x-1}+e^x x^3\right)=n \\ & f(x)=e^x+e^{2 x}+e^{3 x}+\ldots \ldots .+e^{n x}+e^x \cdot x^3 \\ & f^{\prime}(x)=e^x+2 e^{2 x}+3 e^{3 x}+\ldots \ldots .+n e^{n x}+e^x \cdot x^3+e^x \cdot 3 x^2 \\ & f^{\prime \prime}(x)=e^x+2^2 e^{2 x}+3^2 e^{3 x}+\ldots \ldots .+n^2 e^{n x}+e^x \cdot x^3+6 e^x \cdot x^2+e^x \cdot 6 x \\ & f^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=e^x+2^3 e^{2 x}+3^3 e^{3 x}+\ldots \ldots .+n^3 e^{n x}+e^x \cdot x^3+9 e^x \cdot x^2+e^x \cdot 18 x+6 e^x \\ & f^{\prime \prime \prime}(0)=1^3+2^3+3^3+\ldots \ldots .+n^3+6=1302 \\ & \left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^2=1296 \Rightarrow n=8\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad k+n=16$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{l}x^2+a, 0 \leq x \lt 1 \\ 2 x+b, 1 \leq x \leq 2\end{array}\right.$ and $g(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}3 x+b, & 0 \leq x \lt 1 \\ x^3 & , 1 \leq x \leq 2\end{array}\right.$
If derivative of $f(x)$ w.r.t. $g(x)$ at $x=1$ exists, and has value equal to $m$ then the value of $|a+b+6 m|$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{f}^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right)=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{2(1+h)+b-(2+b)}{h}=2$
$f^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1-h)^2+a-(2+b)}{-h}=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\left(h^2-2 h\right)+a-b-1}{-h}=2$
where $a-b-1=0$ $\ldots(1)$
$g^{\prime}\left(1^{-}\right)=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{3(1-h)+b-1}{-h}=3$
where $2+b=0 \Rightarrow b=-2$ $\ldots(2)$
$g^{\prime}\left(1^{+}\right)=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(1+h)^3-1}{h}=\operatorname{Lim}_{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{1+3 h+3 h^2+h^3-1}{h}=3$
from (1) and (2) a = -1; $\frac{\mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{g}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x})}$ at $\mathrm{x}=1=\frac{2}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f(x)= \begin{cases} & \\ (x-a)^2+b, & x \geq k \\ {[2 x],} & 0 < x < k \\ \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right), & x \leq 0\end{cases}$
If $f(x)$ is non-derivable at exactly 6 points, then the value of $a \times b \times k$ is:
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $k$ and $k \in N$.]
questions.solution:
$f(x)$ is non differentiable at $x=0$ and $[2 x]$ is discontinuous at $x=\underbrace{\frac{1}{2}, 1, \frac{3}{2}, 2, \frac{5}{2}, 3}_{5 \text { points }}$
$\therefore f(x)$ should be differentiable and continuous at $x=2$, so $k=3$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f^{\prime}\left(3^{+}\right)=f^{\prime}\left(3^{-}\right) \Rightarrow 2(3-a)=0, a=3 \\
& f\left(3^{+}\right)=f\left(3^{-}\right) \Rightarrow(3-a)^2+b=5, b=5 \\
& a \cdot b \cdot k=3 \times 5 \times 3=45
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ll}\frac{a x^3+b x^2+c x+d}{x}, & x \neq 0 \\ 2, & x=0\end{array}\right.$ be a continuous function where $a, b, c, d$ are in arithmetic progression. Then find the number of points where $|f(|x|)|$ is non derivable.
questions.solution: $$ f(x)= \begin{cases}\frac{a x^3+b x^2+c x+d}{x}, & x \neq 0 \\ 2, & x=0\end{cases} $$ $f(x)$ is continuous $$ \Rightarrow \quad d=0 \text { and } c=2 $$ But $a, b, c$ and $d$ are in A.P. $$ \begin{aligned} \Rightarrow \quad a & =6, b=4, c=2, d=0 \\ f(x) & =\left[\begin{array}{ll} 6 x^2+4 x+2, & x \neq 0 \\ 2, & x=0 \end{array}\right. \\ y & =|f(|x|)| \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The least integral value of $\alpha$ for which the function $f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}x^\alpha\left(\frac{e^{1 / x}-e^{-1 / x}}{e^{1 / x}+e^{-1 / x}}\right), & x \neq 0 \\ 0 & x=0\end{array}\right.$ is differentiable at $x=0$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \text { L.H.D. } & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}(-h)^{\alpha-1}\left(\frac{e^{-2 / h}-1}{e^{-2 / h}+1}\right) \\ \text { R.H.D. } & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0}(h)^{\alpha-1}\left(\frac{1-e^{-2 / h}}{1+e^{2 / h}}\right) \\ \Rightarrow \quad \text { L.H.D. } & =\text { R.H.D. } \\ \Rightarrow \quad \alpha-1 & >0 \\ \Rightarrow & \alpha>1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
